1.text: The burden<S>4853</S> which Habakkuk<S>2265</S> the prophet<S>5030</S> did see<S>2372</S>.
O LORD<S>3068</S>, how long shall I cry<S>7768</S>, and thou wilt not hear<S>8085</S>! even cry out<S>2199</S> unto thee of violence<S>2555</S>, and thou wilt not save<S>3467</S>!

Why dost thou shew<S>7200</S> me iniquity<S>205</S>, and cause me to behold<S>5027</S> grievance<S>5999</S>? for spoiling<S>7701</S> and violence<S>2555</S> are before me: and there are that raise up<S>5375</S> strife<S>7379</S> and contention<S>4066</S>.

Therefore the law<S>8451</S> is slacked<S>6313</S>, and judgment<S>4941</S> doth never<S>5331</S> go forth<S>3318</S>: for the wicked<S>7563</S> doth compass<S>3803</S> about the righteous<S>6662</S>; therefore wrong<S>6127</S> judgment<S>4941</S> proceedeth<S>3318</S>. <sup>wrong: or, wrested</sup>

Behold<S>7200</S> ye among the heathen<S>1471</S>, and regard<S>5027</S>, and wonder<S>8539</S> marvellously<S>8539</S>: for I will work<S>6466</S> a work<S>6467</S> in your days<S>3117</S>, which ye will not believe<S>539</S>, though it be told<S>5608</S> you.

For, lo, I raise up<S>6965</S> the Chaldeans<S>3778</S>, that bitter<S>4751</S> and hasty<S>4116</S> nation<S>1471</S>, which shall march<S>1980</S> through the breadth<S>4800</S> of the land<S>776</S>, to possess<S>3423</S> the dwellingplaces<S>4908</S> that are not theirs. <sup>breadth: Heb. breadths</sup>

They are terrible<S>366</S> and dreadful<S>3372</S>: their judgment<S>4941</S> and their dignity<S>7613</S> shall proceed<S>3318</S> of themselves. <sup>their judgment: or, from them shall proceed the judgment of these, and the captivity of these</sup>

Their horses<S>5483</S> also are swifter<S>7043</S> than the leopards<S>5246</S>, and are more fierce<S>2300</S> than the evening<S>6153</S> wolves<S>2061</S>: and their horsemen<S>6571</S> shall spread<S>6335</S> themselves, and their horsemen<S>6571</S> shall come<S>935</S> from far<S>7350</S>; they shall fly<S>5774</S> as the eagle<S>5404</S> that hasteth<S>2363</S> to eat<S>398</S>. <sup>fierce: Heb. sharp</sup>

They shall come<S>935</S> all for violence<S>2555</S>: their faces<S>6440</S> shall sup up<S>4041</S> as the east wind<S>6921</S>, and they shall gather<S>622</S> the captivity<S>7628</S> as the sand<S>2344</S>. <sup>their: or, the supping up of their faces, etc: or, their faces shall look (Heb. the opposition of their faces) toward the east</sup>

And they shall scoff<S>7046</S> at the kings<S>4428</S>, and the princes<S>7336</S> shall be a scorn<S>4890</S> unto them: they shall deride<S>7832</S> every strong hold<S>4013</S>; for they shall heap<S>6651</S> dust<S>6083</S>, and take<S>3920</S> it.
Then shall his mind<S>7307</S> change<S>2498</S>, and he shall pass over<S>5674</S>, and offend<S>816</S>, imputing this<S>2098</S> his power<S>3581</S> unto his god<S>433</S>.

Art thou not from everlasting<S>6924</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S> my God<S>430</S>, mine Holy One<S>6918</S>? we shall not die<S>4191</S>. O LORD<S>3068</S>, thou hast ordained<S>7760</S> them for judgment<S>4941</S>; and, O mighty God<S>6697</S>, thou hast established<S>3245</S> them for correction<S>3198</S>. <sup>mighty: Heb. rock. established: Heb. founded</sup>

Thou art of purer<S>2889</S> eyes<S>5869</S> than to behold<S>7200</S> evil<S>7451</S>, and canst<S>3201</S> not look<S>5027</S> on iniquity<S>5999</S>: wherefore lookest<S>5027</S> thou upon them that deal treacherously<S>898</S>, and holdest thy tongue<S>2790</S> when the wicked<S>7563</S> devoureth<S>1104</S> the man that is more righteous<S>6662</S> than he? <sup>iniquity: or, grievance</sup>
And makest<S>6213</S> men<S>120</S> as the fishes<S>1709</S> of the sea<S>3220</S>, as the creeping things<S>7431</S>, that have no ruler<S>4910</S> over them? <sup>creeping: or, moving</sup>

They take up<S>5927</S> all of them with the angle<S>2443</S>, they catch<S>1641</S> them in their net<S>2764</S>, and gather<S>622</S> them in their drag<S>4365</S>: therefore they rejoice<S>8055</S> and are glad<S>1523</S>. <sup>drag: or, flue net</sup>

Therefore they sacrifice<S>2076</S> unto their net<S>2764</S>, and burn incense<S>6999</S> unto their drag<S>4365</S>; because by them<S>1992</S> their portion<S>2506</S> is fat<S>8082</S>, and their meat<S>3978</S> plenteous<S>1277</S>. <sup>plenteous: or, dainty: Heb. fat</sup>
Shall they therefore empty<S>7324</S> their net<S>2764</S>, and not spare<S>2550</S> continually<S>8548</S> to slay<S>2026</S> the nations<S>1471</S>?

2.text: I will stand<S>5975</S> upon my watch<S>4931</S>, and set<S>3320</S> me upon the tower<S>4692</S>, and will watch<S>6822</S> to see<S>7200</S> what he will say<S>1696</S> unto me, and what I shall answer<S>7725</S> when I am reproved<S>8433</S>. <sup>tower: Heb. fenced place</sup> <sup>unto me: or, in me</sup> <sup>when: or, when I am argued with: Heb. upon my reproof, or, arguing</sup>

And the LORD<S>3068</S> answered<S>6030</S> me, and said<S>559</S>, Write<S>3789</S> the vision<S>2377</S>, and make it plain<S>874</S> upon tables<S>3871</S>, that he may run<S>7323</S> that readeth<S>7121</S> it.

For the vision<S>2377</S> is yet for an appointed time<S>4150</S>, but at the end<S>7093</S> it shall speak<S>6315</S>, and not lie<S>3576</S>: though it tarry<S>4102</S>, wait<S>2442</S> for it; because it will surely<S>935</S> come<S>935</S>, it will not tarry<S>309</S>.
Behold, his soul<S>5315</S> which is lifted up<S>6075</S> is not upright<S>3474</S> in him: but the just<S>6662</S> shall live<S>2421</S> by his faith<S>530</S>.

Yea also, because he transgresseth<S>898</S> by wine<S>3196</S>, he is a proud<S>3093</S> man<S>1397</S>, neither keepeth at home<S>5115</S>, who enlargeth<S>7337</S> his desire<S>5315</S> as hell<S>7585</S>, and is as death<S>4194</S>, and cannot be satisfied<S>7646</S>, but gathereth<S>622</S> unto him all nations<S>1471</S>, and heapeth<S>6908</S> unto him all people<S>5971</S>: <sup>Yea: or, How much more</sup>

Shall not all these take up<S>5375</S> a parable<S>4912</S> against him, and a taunting<S>4426</S> proverb<S>2420</S> against him, and say<S>559</S>, Woe<S>1945</S> to him that increaseth<S>7235</S> that which is not his! how long? and to him that ladeth<S>3513</S> himself with thick clay<S>5671</S>! <sup>Woe: or, Ho, he</sup>
Shall they not rise up<S>6965</S> suddenly<S>6621</S> that shall bite<S>5391</S> thee, and awake<S>3364</S> that shall vex<S>2111</S> thee, and thou shalt be for booties<S>4933</S> unto them?

Because thou hast spoiled<S>7997</S> many<S>7227</S> nations<S>1471</S>, all the remnant<S>3499</S> of the people<S>5971</S> shall spoil<S>7997</S> thee; because of men's<S>120</S> blood<S>1818</S>, and for the violence<S>2555</S> of the land<S>776</S>, of the city<S>7151</S>, and of all that dwell<S>3427</S> therein. <sup>blood: Heb. bloods</sup>

Woe<S>1945</S> to him that coveteth<S>1214</S> an evil<S>7451</S> covetousness<S>1215</S> to his house<S>1004</S>, that he may set<S>7760</S> his nest<S>7064</S> on high<S>4791</S>, that he may be delivered<S>5337</S> from the power<S>3709</S> of evil<S>7451</S>! <sup>coveteth: or, gaineth and evil gain</sup> <sup>power: Heb. palm of the hand</sup>
Thou hast consulted<S>3289</S> shame<S>1322</S> to thy house<S>1004</S> by cutting off<S>7096</S> many<S>7227</S> people<S>5971</S>, and hast sinned<S>2398</S> against thy soul<S>5315</S>.

For the stone<S>68</S> shall cry out<S>2199</S> of the wall<S>7023</S>, and the beam out<S>3714</S> of the timber<S>6086</S> shall answer<S>6030</S> it. <sup>beam: or, piece, or, fastening</sup> <sup>answer it: or, witness against it</sup>
Woe<S>1945</S> to him that buildeth<S>1129</S> a town<S>5892</S> with blood<S>1818</S>, and stablisheth<S>3559</S> a city<S>7151</S> by iniquity<S>5766</S>! <sup>blood: Heb. bloods</sup>

Behold, is it not of the LORD<S>3068</S> of hosts<S>6635</S> that the people<S>5971</S> shall labour<S>3021</S> in the very<S>1767</S> fire<S>784</S>, and the people<S>3816</S> shall weary<S>3286</S> themselves for very<S>1767</S> vanity<S>7385</S>? <sup>for: or, in vain?</sup>

For the earth<S>776</S> shall be filled<S>4390</S> with the knowledge<S>3045</S> of the glory<S>3519</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>, as the waters<S>4325</S> cover<S>3680</S> the sea<S>3220</S>. <sup>with: or, by knowing the glory</sup>

Woe<S>1945</S> unto him that giveth his neighbour<S>7453</S> drink<S>8248</S>, that puttest<S>5596</S> thy bottle<S>2573</S> to him, and makest him drunken<S>7937</S> also, that thou mayest look<S>5027</S> on their nakedness<S>4589</S>!

Thou art filled<S>7646</S> with shame<S>7036</S> for glory<S>3519</S>: drink<S>8354</S> thou also, and let thy foreskin be uncovered<S>6188</S>: the cup<S>3563</S> of the LORD'S<S>3068</S> right hand<S>3225</S> shall be turned<S>5437</S> unto thee, and shameful spewing<S>7022</S> shall be on thy glory<S>3519</S>. <sup>with: or, more with shame than with glory</sup>

For the violence<S>2555</S> of Lebanon<S>3844</S> shall cover<S>3680</S> thee, and the spoil<S>7701</S> of beasts<S>929</S>, which made them afraid<S>2865</S>, because of men's<S>120</S> blood<S>1818</S>, and for the violence<S>2555</S> of the land<S>776</S>, of the city<S>7151</S>, and of all that dwell<S>3427</S> therein.

What profiteth<S>3276</S> the graven image<S>6459</S> that the maker<S>3335</S> thereof hath graven<S>6458</S> it; the molten image<S>4541</S>, and a teacher<S>3384</S> of lies<S>8267</S>, that the maker<S>3335</S> of his work<S>3336</S> trusteth<S>982</S> therein, to make<S>6213</S> dumb<S>483</S> idols<S>457</S>? <sup>maker of: Heb. fashioner of his fashion</sup>

Woe<S>1945</S> unto him that saith<S>559</S> to the wood<S>6086</S>, Awake<S>6974</S>; to the dumb<S>1748</S> stone<S>68</S>, Arise<S>5782</S>, it shall teach<S>3384</S>! Behold, it is laid<S>8610</S> over with gold<S>2091</S> and silver<S>3701</S>, and there is no breath<S>7307</S> at all in the midst<S>7130</S> of it.
But the LORD<S>3068</S> is in his holy<S>6944</S> temple<S>1964</S>: let all the earth<S>776</S> keep silence<S>2013</S> before<S>6440</S> him. <sup>let: Heb. be silent all the earth before him</sup>

3.text: A prayer<S>8605</S> of Habakkuk<S>2265</S> the prophet<S>5030</S> upon Shigionoth<S>7692</S>. <sup>upon: or, according to variable songs, or, tunes, called in Hebrew, Shigionoth</sup>

O LORD<S>3068</S>, I have heard<S>8085</S> thy speech<S>8088</S>, and was afraid<S>3372</S>: O LORD<S>3068</S>, revive<S>2421</S> thy work<S>6467</S> in the midst<S>7130</S> of the years<S>8141</S>, in the midst<S>7130</S> of the years<S>8141</S> make known<S>3045</S>; in wrath<S>7267</S> remember<S>2142</S> mercy<S>7355</S>. <sup>speech: Heb. report, or, hearing</sup> <sup>revive: or, preserve alive</sup>

God<S>433</S> came<S>935</S> from Teman<S>8487</S>, and the Holy One<S>6918</S> from mount<S>2022</S> Paran<S>6290</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. His glory<S>1935</S> covered<S>3680</S> the heavens<S>8064</S>, and the earth<S>776</S> was full<S>4390</S> of his praise<S>8416</S>. <sup>Teman: or, the south</sup>

And his brightness<S>5051</S> was as the light<S>216</S>; he had horns<S>7161</S> coming out of his hand<S>3027</S>: and there was the hiding<S>2253</S> of his power<S>5797</S>. <sup>horns: or, bright beams out of his side</sup>
Before<S>6440</S> him went<S>3212</S> the pestilence<S>1698</S>, and burning coals<S>7565</S> went forth<S>3318</S> at his feet<S>7272</S>. <sup>coals: or, diseases</sup>

He stood<S>5975</S>, and measured<S>4128</S> the earth<S>776</S>: he beheld<S>7200</S>, and drove asunder<S>5425</S> the nations<S>1471</S>; and the everlasting<S>5703</S> mountains<S>2042</S> were scattered<S>6327</S>, the perpetual<S>5769</S> hills<S>1389</S> did bow<S>7817</S>: his ways<S>1979</S> are everlasting<S>5769</S>.

I saw<S>7200</S> the tents<S>168</S> of Cushan<S>3572</S> in affliction<S>205</S>: and the curtains<S>3407</S> of the land<S>776</S> of Midian<S>4080</S> did tremble<S>7264</S>. <sup>Cushan: or, Ethiopia</sup> <sup>in: or, under affliction, or, vanity</sup>

Was the LORD<S>3068</S> displeased<S>2734</S> against the rivers<S>5104</S>? was thine anger<S>639</S> against the rivers<S>5104</S>? was thy wrath<S>5678</S> against the sea<S>3220</S>, that thou didst ride<S>7392</S> upon thine horses<S>5483</S> and thy chariots<S>4818</S> of salvation<S>3444</S>? <sup>of: or, were salvation?</sup>

Thy bow<S>7198</S> was made quite<S>6181</S> naked<S>5783</S>, according to the oaths<S>7621</S> of the tribes<S>4294</S>, even thy word<S>562</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. Thou didst cleave<S>1234</S> the earth<S>776</S> with rivers<S>5104</S>. <sup>earth: or, rivers of the earth</sup>

The mountains<S>2022</S> saw<S>7200</S> thee, and they trembled<S>2342</S>: the overflowing<S>2230</S> of the water<S>4325</S> passed by<S>5674</S>: the deep<S>8415</S> uttered<S>5414</S> his voice<S>6963</S>, and lifted up<S>5375</S> his hands<S>3027</S> on high<S>7315</S>.

The sun<S>8121</S> and moon<S>3394</S> stood still<S>5975</S> in their habitation<S>2073</S>: at the light<S>216</S> of thine arrows<S>2671</S> they went<S>1980</S>, and at the shining<S>5051</S> of thy glittering<S>1300</S> spear<S>2595</S>. <sup>at the light: or, thine arrows walked in the light</sup>
Thou didst march through<S>6805</S> the land<S>776</S> in indignation<S>2195</S>, thou didst thresh<S>1758</S> the heathen<S>1471</S> in anger<S>639</S>.

Thou wentest forth<S>3318</S> for the salvation<S>3468</S> of thy people<S>5971</S>, even for salvation<S>3468</S> with thine anointed<S>4899</S>; thou woundedst<S>4272</S> the head<S>7218</S> out of the house<S>1004</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S>, by discovering<S>6168</S> the foundation<S>3247</S> unto the neck<S>6677</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>by: Heb. making naked</sup>

Thou didst strike through<S>5344</S> with his staves<S>4294</S> the head<S>7218</S> of his villages<S>6518</S>: they came out as a whirlwind<S>5590</S> to scatter<S>6327</S> me: their rejoicing<S>5951</S> was as to devour<S>398</S> the poor<S>6041</S> secretly<S>4565</S>. <sup>came: Heb. were tempestuous</sup>
Thou didst walk<S>1869</S> through the sea<S>3220</S> with thine horses<S>5483</S>, through the heap<S>2563</S> of great<S>7227</S> waters<S>4325</S>. <sup>heap: or, mud</sup>

When I heard<S>8085</S>, my belly<S>990</S> trembled<S>7264</S>; my lips<S>8193</S> quivered<S>6750</S> at the voice<S>6963</S>: rottenness<S>7538</S> entered<S>935</S> into my bones<S>6106</S>, and I trembled<S>7264</S> in myself, that I might rest<S>5117</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of trouble<S>6869</S>: when he cometh up<S>5927</S> unto the people<S>5971</S>, he will invade them with his troops<S>1464</S>. <sup>invade: or, cut them in pieces</sup>

Although the fig tree<S>8384</S> shall not blossom<S>6524</S>, neither shall fruit<S>2981</S> be in the vines<S>1612</S>; the labour<S>4639</S> of the olive<S>2132</S> shall fail<S>3584</S>, and the fields<S>7709</S> shall yield<S>6213</S> no meat<S>400</S>; the flock<S>6629</S> shall be cut off<S>1504</S> from the fold<S>4356</S>, and there shall be no herd<S>1241</S> in the stalls<S>7517</S>: <sup>fail: Heb. lie</sup>
Yet I will rejoice<S>5937</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S>, I will joy<S>1523</S> in the God<S>430</S> of my salvation<S>3468</S>.

The LORD<S>3069</S> God<S>136</S> is my strength<S>2428</S>, and he will make<S>7760</S> my feet<S>7272</S> like hinds'<S>355</S> feet, and he will make me to walk<S>1869</S> upon mine high places<S>1116</S>. To the chief singer<S>5329</S> on my stringed instruments<S>5058</S>. <sup>stringed: Heb. Neginoth</sup>
